title: craspedaria squamulosa
intro: Craspedaria squamulosa, also known as the 'Rough Tree Fern', is a species of fern found in Australia, characterized by its distinctive, scaly fronds. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Craspedaria squamulosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, teas, and topical applications, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
